provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (multi-agent authored + adversarially verified; AU/AMH context; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.
provenance_defaults.authored_by: claude-fable-5 workflow (author+verify agents; draft — KL to attest)

records.rationale: Titrated to clinical response (heart rate, blood pressure) rather than plasma levels; not an NTI drug
Wide therapeutic margin; managed by clinical/blood-pressure and electrolyte response, not plasma level monitoring
Not a narrow-therapeutic-index drug; managed by electrolyte and clinical monitoring rather than drug levels.
Dosed to thyroid function tests, not plasma drug levels; not a narrow-therapeutic-index agent
Topical ocular lubricant with negligible systemic exposure
Topical ocular lubricant with negligible systemic exposure
Anticonvulsant with an established therapeutic reference range and dose-related CNS/haematological toxicity; plasma-level monitoring is used to guide dosing and assess adherence
records.monitoring_hint: Trough plasma ethosuximide level; check after dose changes and if breakthrough seizures or toxicity

records.rationale: mTOR inhibitor with concentration-dependent efficacy and toxicity and marked CYP3A4/P-gp-mediated interaction potential; whole-blood trough monitoring is standard in transplantation and tuberous sclerosis indications
records.monitoring_hint: Whole-blood trough concentration; recheck after dose changes, interacting drugs or hepatic impairment

records.rationale: Inhaled bronchodilator titrated to clinical response; does not require serum level monitoring
Dosed to clinical and biochemical response; not a narrow-therapeutic-index drug requiring level monitoring
Lipid-lowering agent monitored by lipid profile, not plasma drug levels
Titrated to blood glucose response; no plasma level monitoring, though hypoglycaemia risk warrants clinical vigilance
Titrated to blood glucose response; no plasma level monitoring required
Wide therapeutic margin; no routine plasma level monitoring required.
Antibiotic dosed empirically; no level monitoring required.
Titrated to clinical response and tolerability; plasma levels not routinely monitored.
Wide safety margin; no therapeutic drug monitoring required.
Titrated to symptom control; no narrow-index level monitoring required.
Thyroid hormone with a narrow margin between under- and over-replacement; small dosing changes can precipitate cardiac effects, and T3 has a faster, less forgiving onset than levothyroxine
records.therapeutic_interval: Titrated to biochemical euthyroidism rather than a serum drug-level range
records.monitoring_hint: TSH and free T4, with clinical/cardiac assessment; monitored biochemically rather than by serum drug concentration

records.rationale: Immunosuppressant with a narrow therapeutic index where subtherapeutic levels risk rejection and supratherapeutic levels cause toxicity; requires whole-blood trough monitoring
records.therapeutic_interval: Whole-blood trough concentration target set by transplant indication and concomitant immunosuppression (commonly in the region of 4-15 ng/mL depending on protocol)
records.monitoring_hint: Trough whole-blood concentration by validated immunoassay/LC-MS, timed relative to dose and reviewed after any interacting-drug change

records.rationale: Dosed to clinical effect and tolerability rather than plasma level monitoring; not a level-guided narrow-therapeutic-index agent
Water-soluble vitamin with a wide safety margin; excess is renally excreted
Titrated to clinical response; routine plasma level monitoring is not established
Fixed-dose antiplatelet without therapeutic level monitoring
Dosed to clinical endpoint (IOP, heart rate, blood pressure); not level-monitored
Wide therapeutic margin; dosed to clinical response without blood-level monitoring
Non-absorbed phosphate binder titrated to serum phosphate, not a level-monitored NTI drug
Symptom-titrated alpha-blocker without level monitoring
Dosed to lipid targets; not level-monitored and has a wide therapeutic margin
Wide therapeutic margin; glucose-dependent action with low hypoglycaemia risk. No plasma-level monitoring; managed by renal-function-based dosing and HbA1c/glucose response.
Not an NTI drug; no therapeutic drug monitoring. Safety governed by renal function and lactic-acidosis risk factors rather than a narrow target level.
Not an NTI drug; titrated to clinical effect. Serum potassium and renal function are monitored for safety, not drug levels.
Locally acting, minimally absorbed phosphate binder; titrated to serum phosphate. No systemic therapeutic window.
Not a level-monitored NTI drug; dosed clinically with FBC/LFT safety monitoring. Acetylator status influences toxicity but routine level monitoring is not used.

Aminoglycoside with a narrow margin between effective and toxic systemic exposure; excessive trough concentrations cause cumulative nephrotoxicity and irreversible ototoxicity, requiring therapeutic drug monitoring for systemic use
records.therapeutic_interval: Regimen-dependent target concentrations; low trough targets with peak or AUC monitoring
records.monitoring_hint: Monitor serum concentrations (trough +/- peak or AUC), renal function and, where prolonged, audiometry/vestibular function
